sections.heading: Benefit amount
sections.text: Under the Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN) scheme, eligible farmer families receive ₹6,000 per year. This is paid in three equal installments of ₹2,000 every four months, transferred directly into the beneficiary's bank account through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT). No intermediary is involved in the disbursement.

sections.heading: Eligibility
sections.text: All landholding farmer families, subject to certain exclusion criteria, are eligible for the benefit. The family is defined as husband, wife and minor children who own cultivable land. Land ownership records as on the cut-off date determine eligibility.

sections.heading: Exclusions
sections.text: Institutional landholders and farmer families holding constitutional posts are excluded. Serving or retired government employees, income-tax payers in the last assessment year, and professionals such as doctors, engineers and lawyers who are also practising are not eligible. Pensioners drawing a monthly pension of ₹10,000 or more (excluding Group D staff) are also excluded.

sections.heading: How to register
sections.text: Farmers can self-register through the PM-KISAN portal or the PM-KISAN mobile app, or apply at the nearest Common Service Centre (CSC). Aadhaar linking and eKYC are mandatory to receive installments. Beneficiaries can check their payment status online using their registered mobile number or Aadhaar.

title: Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY: Health Coverage
department: Ministry of Health & Family Welfare / National Health Authority

sections.heading: Coverage amount
sections.text: Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PM-JAY) provides health cover of ₹5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary hospitalisation. There is no cap on family size or age, and the cover is available on a family floater basis. The benefit is fully cashless and paperless at empanelled hospitals.

sections.heading: Who is covered
sections.text: The scheme targets poor and vulnerable families identified through the Socio-Economic Caste Census (SECC) 2011 deprivation and occupational criteria. In 2024, coverage was extended to all senior citizens aged 70 years and above, irrespective of income, under the Ayushman Vay Vandana card. More than 12 crore families are covered nationwide.

sections.heading: What is included
sections.text: The package covers pre-hospitalisation up to 3 days and post-hospitalisation expenses up to 15 days, including diagnostics and medicines. Over 1,900 medical procedures across specialties such as cardiology, oncology and orthopaedics are covered. Pre-existing conditions are covered from day one of enrolment.

sections.heading: How to avail
sections.text: Beneficiaries can verify eligibility on the PM-JAY portal or by calling the toll-free helpline 14555. An Ayushman card can be generated free of cost at empanelled hospitals or CSCs using Aadhaar-based verification. Treatment is provided cashless at any empanelled public or private hospital across India.

sections.heading: Interest subsidy
sections.text: Under the Credit Linked Subsidy Scheme (CLSS) of Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (Urban), eligible beneficiaries receive an interest subsidy on home loans. Economically Weaker Section (EWS) and Low Income Group (LIG) households get a 6.5% interest subsidy on loans up to ₹6 lakh for a tenure of 20 years. The maximum subsidy works out to around ₹2.67 lakh, credited upfront to reduce the loan principal.

sections.heading: Income eligibility
sections.text: EWS households have an annual income up to ₹3 lakh, while LIG households earn between ₹3 lakh and ₹6 lakh per year. Middle Income Group MIG-I covers incomes up to ₹12 lakh and MIG-II up to ₹18 lakh, with subsidies of 4% and 3% respectively. The beneficiary family must not own a pucca house anywhere in India.

sections.heading: House and beneficiary conditions
sections.text: The scheme mandates that the house should preferably be in the name of the woman head of the household or jointly. Beneficiary families comprise husband, wife and unmarried children. The carpet area limits are 30 sq m for EWS and 60 sq m for LIG dwellings under the credit-linked component.

sections.heading: How to apply
sections.text: Applications can be filed online on the PMAY-U portal or through the CSC network for a nominal fee. Applicants must provide Aadhaar and income documents at the time of loan sanction. The lending institution processes the subsidy claim through the National Housing Bank or HUDCO as the central nodal agency.

title: How to File an RTI Application

sections.heading: Application fee
sections.text: The prescribed fee for filing a Right to Information (RTI) application with a central public authority is ₹10, payable by cash, demand draft, banker's cheque or online payment. Citizens below the poverty line (BPL) are exempted from paying any fee on producing proof. Additional charges of ₹2 per page apply for photocopies of information supplied.

sections.heading: Where to apply
sections.text: Applications can be filed online through the RTI Online portal for central ministries and departments, or physically with the concerned Public Information Officer (PIO). Each public authority designates a PIO to receive and respond to requests. State-specific RTI portals exist for many state government departments.

sections.heading: Response timeline
sections.text: The PIO must provide the information within 30 days of receipt of the application. Where the information concerns the life or liberty of a person, it must be furnished within 48 hours. If the application is transferred to another authority, an additional 5 days may be added to the timeline.

sections.heading: Appeals
sections.text: If information is denied or not received in time, the applicant can file a first appeal with the First Appellate Authority within 30 days. A second appeal can be filed with the Central or State Information Commission within 90 days of the first appeal decision. No fee is charged for filing appeals under the RTI Act, 2005.

sections.heading: Entitlement
sections.text: Under the National Food Security Act (NFSA), 2013, Priority Household (PHH) beneficiaries are entitled to 5 kg of foodgrains per person per month. Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) households receive 35 kg of foodgrains per family per month. Since 2023, these foodgrains are provided free of cost under the Pradhan Mantri Garib Kalyan Anna Yojana.

sections.heading: Who can apply
sections.text: Any Indian citizen who does not already hold a ration card can apply, with eligibility based on the household's economic status. The card is issued in the name of the eldest woman of the household aged 18 years or above as the head of the family. Applicants must not be enrolled on any other state's ration card.

sections.heading: Documents required
sections.text: Applicants need proof of identity and address such as Aadhaar, voter ID or utility bills, along with a passport-size photograph of the head of the family. Details of all family members with their Aadhaar numbers are required for seeding. A self-declaration of income may be needed to determine the card category.

sections.heading: Application process
sections.text: Applications are submitted through the state's food and civil supplies department portal or at the local ration/PDS office. After verification by the concerned authority, the card is typically issued within 15 to 30 days. One Nation One Ration Card portability allows beneficiaries to draw rations from any fair price shop across India.

title: Birth Certificate: Registration Process
department: Office of the Registrar General of India

sections.heading: Registration timeline
sections.text: Under the Registration of Births and Deaths Act, every birth must be registered within 21 days of its occurrence free of charge. Registration after 21 days but within 30 days requires a late fee, and beyond 30 days up to one year requires written permission from the prescribed authority. Births reported after one year can only be registered on an order of a magistrate along with the prescribed fee.

sections.heading: Where to register
sections.text: Births occurring in hospitals are usually reported by the institution to the local registrar automatically. For home births, the head of the household must report to the local municipal body or gram panchayat registrar. Many states now allow online registration and download of digitally signed certificates through the Civil Registration System (CRS) portal.

sections.heading: Documents required
sections.text: Proof of birth from the hospital, identity and address proof of parents, and their Aadhaar numbers are generally required. The 2023 amendment makes the digital birth certificate a single document to prove date and place of birth for admissions, driving licences and other services. There is no application fee for on-time registration.

sections.heading: Corrections
sections.text: Errors in the registered certificate, such as spelling of names, can be corrected by applying to the issuing registrar with supporting documents. A name can be added within the record after registration within the period allowed by state rules. Corrections after issuance may require an affidavit and a nominal processing fee.

title: PM Ujjwala Yojana: Free LPG Connection

sections.heading: What is provided
sections.text: The Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY) provides a free LPG connection with a deposit-free cylinder and regulator to women from eligible households. The government provides financial assistance of ₹1,600 per connection to cover the security deposit and fitting charges. Beneficiaries also receive the first refill and a gas stove free of cost under PMUY 2.0.

sections.heading: Subsidy on refills
sections.text: PMUY beneficiaries receive a targeted subsidy of ₹300 per 14.2 kg LPG cylinder, for up to 12 refills in a year. The subsidy is credited directly to the beneficiary's bank account through DBT after purchase. This support is aimed at making clean cooking fuel affordable for low-income families.

sections.heading: Eligibility
sections.text: The connection is issued in the name of an adult woman belonging to a poor household who does not already have an LPG connection. Eligible categories include SC/ST households, PMAY beneficiaries, Antyodaya families, forest dwellers and the most backward classes. Self-declaration of family composition and address is accepted under PMUY 2.0 for migrants.

sections.heading: How to apply
sections.text: Applications can be made online through the PMUY portal by choosing the preferred oil marketing company such as Indane, Bharat Gas or HP Gas. Applicants submit Aadhaar, a bank account, and a ration card or self-declaration. The connection is delivered by the local LPG distributor after KYC verification.

title: National Scholarship: Eligibility
department: Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment / Ministry of Education

sections.heading: Post-Matric Scholarship for SC students
sections.text: The Post-Matric Scholarship provides financial assistance to SC students whose parental annual income does not exceed ₹2.5 lakh. It covers full tuition and non-refundable fees along with a monthly maintenance allowance that varies by course group. The scholarship is available for studies from Class 11 up to postgraduate and professional courses.

sections.heading: Pre-Matric Scholarship
sections.text: The Pre-Matric Scholarship supports students of SC, ST and other eligible categories studying in Classes 9 and 10. The parental income ceiling is ₹2.5 lakh per annum, and the benefit includes an academic allowance and maintenance support. Day scholars and hostellers receive different rates of assistance.

sections.heading: Merit-cum-Means and minority scholarships
sections.text: Merit-cum-Means Scholarship for professional and technical courses is available to students from minority communities with family income up to ₹2.5 lakh per year. It reimburses course fees subject to a ceiling and provides a maintenance allowance for the duration of the course. A fixed percentage of scholarships is reserved for girl students.

sections.heading: How to apply
sections.text: All applications are submitted through the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) within the notified window, usually between July and October. Students must complete Aadhaar-based verification and one-time registration to generate a permanent NSP ID. Institutions verify the applications before they are approved by the state or ministry.

title: Municipal Property Tax: Assessment and Payment

sections.heading: What is property tax
sections.text: Property tax is an annual levy imposed by urban local bodies on owners of residential, commercial and vacant land within municipal limits. It is a primary source of revenue used to fund civic services such as roads, drainage, street lighting and sanitation. The tax is usually assessed for the financial year running from 1 April to 31 March.

sections.heading: How it is calculated
sections.text: Most municipalities calculate property tax using the Unit Area Value or Capital Value system, based on the built-up area, location zone, usage type and age of the building. The applicable rate per square metre is multiplied by these factors to arrive at the annual tax. Self-occupied residential properties often attract lower rates than commercial or rented ones.

sections.heading: Rebates and penalties
sections.text: Many corporations offer an early-payment rebate, commonly around 5% to 10%, for taxpayers who clear their dues within the first quarter of the year. Senior citizens, women owners and green buildings may qualify for additional concessions in some cities. Late payment typically attracts interest or penalty of around 1% to 2% per month on the outstanding amount.

sections.heading: How to pay
sections.text: Property tax can be paid online through the municipal corporation's portal using the unique Property Identification Number (PID) or assessment number. Payment is also accepted at citizen service centres and ward offices. On successful payment, a digital receipt is generated that serves as proof of tax clearance.

sections.heading: Applying for a new electricity connection
sections.text: A new domestic electricity connection can be applied for online through the state discom's portal or at the local sub-division office. Applicants must submit proof of identity, proof of ownership or occupancy, and a recent photograph. Most state utilities are mandated to release a metered connection within 7 days in urban areas after payment of charges.

sections.heading: Electricity connection charges
sections.text: The applicant pays a security deposit, application and processing fee, and meter cost, which vary by state and sanctioned load. A single-phase domestic connection generally requires a smaller deposit than a three-phase or commercial connection. A test report from a licensed electrical contractor may be required for the internal wiring.

sections.heading: Applying for a new water connection
sections.text: A new water supply connection is obtained from the municipal water board by submitting an application with property ownership documents and a site plan. The applicant pays connection charges, a security deposit and road-cutting charges where applicable. After inspection, the pipeline tap connection and water meter are installed by the board's authorised plumber.

sections.heading: Timelines and tracking
sections.text: Under many state Right to Services Acts, a new water connection must be provided within a defined period, often 15 to 30 days, from a complete application. Applicants receive a service request number to track the status online. Delays beyond the notified timeline can be escalated to the designated appellate officer.

sections.heading: Pension amount
sections.text: Under the Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (IGNOAPS), part of the National Social Assistance Programme (NSAP), a central pension of ₹200 per month is provided to beneficiaries aged 60 to 79 years. For beneficiaries aged 80 years and above, the central contribution rises to ₹500 per month. Most states top up this amount, so the total monthly pension varies considerably across states.

sections.heading: Eligibility
sections.text: The applicant must be aged 60 years or above and belong to a family living below the poverty line as per the prescribed criteria. The pension is meant for destitute elderly persons with little or no regular means of subsistence. Applicants already receiving another old-age pension are generally not eligible for a duplicate benefit.

sections.heading: How to apply
sections.text: Applications are submitted to the local gram panchayat, block office or municipal ward office, or online through state social welfare portals. Required documents include age proof, a BPL certificate or ration card, Aadhaar and bank account details. The pension is credited monthly through DBT into the beneficiary's bank or post office account.

title: PM SVANidhi: Loan for Street Vendors

sections.heading: Loan amounts
sections.text: The PM Street Vendor's AtmaNirbhar Nidhi (PM SVANidhi) scheme offers collateral-free working capital loans to urban street vendors in three tiers. The first loan is up to ₹10,000, the second up to ₹20,000 on timely repayment of the first, and the third up to ₹50,000. Each loan is repayable in equated monthly installments over 12 months.

sections.heading: Interest subsidy and incentives
sections.text: Vendors who repay their loans regularly are eligible for an interest subsidy of 7% per annum, credited to their accounts on a quarterly basis. There is no penalty for early repayment of the loan. This structure rewards timely repayment and encourages vendors to build a formal credit history.

sections.heading: Digital transaction cashback
sections.text: To promote digital payments, the scheme provides a cashback of up to ₹100 per month to vendors who use digital transactions such as UPI or QR codes. Cashback is given at ₹1 per eligible transaction across defined slabs. This incentivises street vendors to adopt cashless collections.

sections.heading: Eligibility and application
sections.text: Street vendors who were vending in urban areas on or before 24 March 2020 and hold a Certificate of Vending or an equivalent identity are eligible. Vendors identified in surveys but left out, and those vending in surrounding peri-urban and rural areas, are also covered. Applications can be filed through the PM SVANidhi portal, mobile app, CSCs or participating lending institutions.

title: PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana

sections.heading: Benefit
sections.text: Under PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana, households installing rooftop solar receive a Central Financial Assistance subsidy of 30,000 rupees per kW for the first 2 kW and 18,000 rupees per kW for the third kW, capped at 78,000 rupees for systems of 3 kW and above. Households can get up to 300 units of free electricity per month.

sections.heading: Eligibility
sections.text: The applicant must be an Indian citizen, own a house with a roof suitable for solar panels, and have a valid electricity connection. The household must not have availed any other solar subsidy for the same installation.

sections.heading: How to apply
sections.text: Register on the national portal pmsuryaghar.gov.in with your electricity consumer number and mobile, apply for feasibility approval, get the system installed by a registered vendor, and the subsidy is credited to your bank account after net-meter installation and inspection.

title: Ayushman Bharat Vay Vandana for Senior Citizens

sections.heading: Benefit
sections.text: All senior citizens aged 70 years and above are entitled to free health cover of up to 5 lakh rupees per year under the Ayushman Vay Vandana expansion of AB PM-JAY, irrespective of their income. Families already covered under PM-JAY get an additional top-up of 5 lakh rupees per year exclusively for senior members.

sections.heading: Eligibility
sections.text: Any citizen aged 70 or above is eligible regardless of socio-economic status. A dedicated Vay Vandana card is issued on Aadhaar-based e-KYC.

sections.heading: How to apply
sections.text: Enrol on the PM-JAY portal or app using Aadhaar; a distinct Vay Vandana card is generated for the 70+ member.

title: PM Kaushal Vikas Yojana - Free Skill Training

sections.heading: Benefit
sections.text: PMKVY provides free short-term skill training (typically 200-600 hours) across sectors, with National Skills Qualification Framework certification recognised by employers. Training includes soft skills and, in many centres, placement assistance.

sections.heading: Eligibility
sections.text: Indian citizens aged 15 to 45 who are school or college dropouts or unemployed youth are eligible. No fee is charged to the candidate.

sections.heading: How to apply
sections.text: Register at a Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Kendra or on the Skill India Digital portal, choose a job role and centre, and complete enrolment with Aadhaar and a bank account.

sections.heading: Benefit
sections.text: Registered artisans receive a toolkit incentive of 15,000 rupees, a stipend during skill training of 500 rupees per day, and collateral-free enterprise loans of up to 1 lakh rupees in the first tranche and 2 lakh rupees in the second, at a concessional 5% interest rate.

sections.heading: Eligibility
sections.text: The scheme covers 18 traditional trades including carpenter, blacksmith, goldsmith, potter, cobbler, tailor, barber, and boat maker. The applicant must be engaged in the trade and aged 18 or above.

sections.heading: How to apply
sections.text: Enrol through a Common Service Centre with Aadhaar-based biometric verification; registration is free.

sections.heading: Benefit
sections.text: The Kisan Credit Card gives farmers short-term crop loans up to 3 lakh rupees at an effective interest rate of 4% per annum when repaid on time, thanks to interest subvention and a prompt-repayment incentive. It also covers post-harvest and consumption needs.

sections.heading: Eligibility
sections.text: All farmers - individual or joint owner-cultivators, tenant farmers, oral lessees, and sharecroppers - as well as those in animal husbandry and fisheries are eligible.

sections.heading: How to apply
sections.text: Apply at any commercial bank, cooperative bank, or regional rural bank with land records, Aadhaar, and a passport photo. Applications for KCC on the PM-KISAN portal are also accepted.

sections.heading: Benefit
sections.text: Selected interns receive a monthly stipend of 5,000 rupees - 4,500 from the government and 500 from the company - plus a one-time grant of 6,000 rupees. Internships run for 12 months in India's top companies.

sections.heading: Eligibility
sections.text: Candidates aged 21 to 24 who are not in full-time employment or full-time education are eligible. Family annual income must not exceed 8 lakh rupees, and no family member should be a permanent government employee.

sections.heading: How to apply
sections.text: Register on the PM Internship portal, complete your profile, and apply to listed opportunities matched to your qualifications and location.

sections.heading: Benefit
sections.text: NMMSS awards 12,000 rupees per year (1,000 rupees per month) to selected students from class 9 to class 12 to reduce dropout at the secondary stage.

sections.heading: Eligibility
sections.text: Students whose parental income does not exceed 3.5 lakh rupees per year and who have cleared the state-level selection test in class 8, with at least 55% marks (relaxation for reserved categories), are eligible.

sections.heading: How to apply
sections.text: Apply on the National Scholarship Portal after clearing the state selection examination, and renew each year subject to academic progress.

sections.heading: Benefit
sections.text: Street vendors get collateral-free working-capital loans starting at 10,000 rupees, rising to 20,000 and then 50,000 rupees on timely repayment. A 7% interest subsidy and cashback on digital transactions are provided.

sections.heading: Eligibility
sections.text: Urban street vendors who were vending on or before 24 March 2020 and hold a Certificate of Vending or are identified in the survey are eligible.

sections.heading: How to apply
sections.text: Apply through the PM SVANidhi portal, a bank, or a Common Service Centre with your vending certificate and Aadhaar.

title: One Nation One Ration Card
department: Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution

sections.heading: Benefit
sections.text: One Nation One Ration Card lets NFSA beneficiaries lift their entitled subsidised foodgrain from any Fair Price Shop anywhere in India using biometric authentication, making the ration card portable across states.

sections.heading: Eligibility
sections.text: Any existing ration card holder under the National Food Security Act is automatically covered; no separate application is needed.

sections.heading: How to apply
sections.text: Ensure your ration card is seeded with Aadhaar. At any FPS in any state, authenticate biometrically to draw your quota.

sections.heading: Benefit
sections.text: The Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises enables banks to extend collateral-free and third-party-guarantee-free credit up to 5 crore rupees to eligible micro and small enterprises, with the guarantee cover borne by the trust.

sections.heading: Eligibility
sections.text: New and existing micro and small enterprises engaged in manufacturing or services (excluding retail trade in some cases) are eligible; the lending institution must be a member of CGTMSE.

sections.heading: How to apply
sections.text: Approach a member bank or financial institution for a business loan and request coverage under the CGTMSE guarantee scheme.
